text: The surgeon performs primary closure of the perforated pre-pyloric gastric ulcer using multiple intra-corporeal sutures. The closure is reinforced with a second layer of sutures. Intra-abdominal fluid is drained, and the abdomen is irrigated with an antibiotic solution. The omental attachments to the transverse colon are mobilized to create an omental patch, which is used to reinforce the closure. The integrity of the closure is confirmed by inflating the stomach with diluted blue dye via a nasogastric tube, showing no dye extravasation. The area is then reinforced with a patch of greater omentum.
